title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.articleTitle: The Architecture of the Roman Aqueducts
content.text: The Roman aqueducts (0) stand as one of the most impressive feats of ancient civil engineering. Built to transport fresh water over vast distances, these structures (1) the growth of large cities throughout the Roman Empire. The secret to their success (2) in the Romans' mastery of the arch and the use of a slight, consistent downward slope. Engineers had to be (3) precise in their calculations to ensure that water flowed steadily without stagnating or causing erosion. While many people (4) aqueducts with the towering stone bridges seen in the countryside, most of the network was actually hidden underground in pipes and tunnels. This helped to keep the water cool and (5) it from contamination and theft. Maintenance was a (6) task, requiring a dedicated workforce to clear debris and repair leaks regularly. The impact of these systems on public health was (7) , providing clean water for drinking, public baths, and sanitation. Even today, several of these ancient structures are still in (8) , a testament to the durability and ingenuity of Roman construction techniques that allowed their civilization to thrive for centuries across diverse and challenging terrains of the European and Mediterranean landscapes.

example.explanation: “Stand as” is a common figurative expression meaning to serve as a symbol or example.

questions.explanation: “Facilitated” is a formal verb meaning to make an action or process easier.
“The secret lay in” is the past tense of the fixed expression ‘to lie in something’.
“Extremely precise” is a natural adverb-adjective collocation.
“Associate with” is used when people connect one thing with another in their minds.
“Protect from” is the standard verb for preventing harm or contamination.
“Constant task” describes something that requires continuous attention.
“Immense” is used to describe something very large or great in scale or impact.
“In use” is a fixed prepositional phrase meaning currently being utilized.
